dimensions.rationale: Tremfya faces persistent step-edit headwinds at major PBMs. CVS/Caremark and Express Scripts continue to favor Skyrizi and Cosentyx on preferred formulary tiers in several large commercial plans, requiring prior authorization and step-through TNF failure for Tremfya access. NICE approval is secured but UK uptake is capped by regional ICS budget pressures. CMS Part D coverage is stable but IRA best-price implications constrain rebate flexibility for 2026 contracting cycles. PsA-specific formulary positioning remains weaker than plaque psoriasis — many plans still default to IL-17 agents as first-line biologic in PsA, limiting Tremfya pull-through in rheumatology. Step-edit reversal rate is below 50% in top-20 commercial plans, directly suppressing biologic-naive capture.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Accelerate Q3 2026 PBM contract renegotiations targeting Tier 2 preferred status for Tremfya in PsA; deploy HEOR dossier update with 4-year durability and total cost-of-care modeling showing Q8W dosing advantage vs. Skyrizi Q12W and IL-17 Q4W. Owner: Market Access. Urgency: Immediate. KPI: Preferred formulary coverage increase from 42% to 55% of commercial lives by Q4 2026.

dimensions.rationale: Long-term safety data through 4+ years is a genuine differentiator and MSL teams are effectively disseminating VOYAGE and DISCOVER extension data. However, head-to-head evidence amplification against Skyrizi remains underpowered — the GUIDE study (Tremfya vs. Skyrizi in PsO) data has not yet been fully leveraged in rheumatology congresses, and rheumatology KOL alignment lags dermatology. MSL coverage in top-50 rheumatology accounts is only at 60% saturation. Medical education programming on IL-23 class differentiation (selective p19 vs. dual IL-12/23) is mature but needs refresh to counter AbbVie's aggressive Skyrizi real-world evidence narrative. No current gaps in publication strategy but competitive share-of-voice at ACR and EULAR is being eroded by Skyrizi-funded symposia.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Deploy 8 additional MSLs to top-25 underserved rheumatology academic centers by Q3 2026; submit GUIDE PsA subgroup analysis abstract to ACR 2026 by June 1 deadline; commission independent network meta-analysis comparing Tremfya vs. Skyrizi in PsA joint and skin co-primary endpoints. Owner: Medical Affairs. Urgency: Near-term. KPI: Rheumatology KOL engagement index increase from 60% to 80% account saturation; accepted ACR abstract by August 2026.

dimensions.rationale: Brand positioning in plaque psoriasis is well-established around selective IL-23 inhibition and durable Q8W dosing. However, the PsA growth mandate is underserved by current promotional materials — field feedback indicates reps lack rheumatology-specific detail aids that address joint progression endpoints and musculoskeletal imaging data. The IL-23 vs. IL-17 positioning narrative is strong conceptually but competitive response from Novartis (Cosentyx/SEC data in axial SpA) and Lilly (Taltz IL-17A positioning) is intensifying. Biologic-naive patient capture campaigns via DTC are performing below benchmark: aided awareness among PsA patients not yet on biologics is 28% vs. 41% for Skyrizi. The Q8W adherence messaging is approved and differentiated but is buried in secondary messaging hierarchy — it should be elevated given real-world adherence data showing 15% higher persistence vs. IL-17 class at 12 months.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Launch PsA-specific detail aid and leave-behind by June 2026 incorporating DISCOVER-1/2 joint progression data; elevate Q8W adherence and persistence advantage to primary message position in all rheumatology-facing materials; increase DTC investment in PsA-specific digital channels by 30% targeting biologic-naive patients with CTA to HCP discussion guide. Owner: Marketing. Urgency: Immediate. KPI: Biologic-naive PsA aided awareness to 38% by Q4 2026; PsA new-to-brand Rx share increase from 14% to 18%.

dimensions.rationale: Field force alignment in dermatology is mature and performing well — Tremfya maintains consistent top-3 share of voice among dermatologists. However, rheumatology field coverage is structurally weaker: the immunology sales team covers rheumatology as a secondary call target behind dermatology, resulting in lower call frequency to high-decile rheumatologists. CRM data shows average rheumatology HCP touches at 2.1 per quarter vs. 4.8 for dermatology. Incentive compensation plans do not differentially weight PsA prescriptions despite the strategic growth mandate. Hub and reimbursement support teams report increased PsA prior authorization volumes but have maintained turnaround targets. Sales-medical alignment meetings are occurring quarterly but need to shift to monthly given competitive acceleration.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Restructure Q3 2026 incentive compensation to double-weight PsA new-starts vs. PsO maintenance; increase rheumatology call frequency target to 4.0 per quarter for top-200 rheumatologists; shift sales-medical alignment cadence to monthly with Skyrizi competitive intelligence standing agenda item. Owner: Commercial Ops. Urgency: Near-term. KPI: Rheumatology call frequency to 4.0/quarter by Q4 2026; PsA new-start contribution to total Tremfya Rx growth at 25%.

dimensions.rationale: Tremfya has a clean regulatory profile — no REMS, no boxed warning, and a well-characterized safety database through 4+ years of post-marketing experience. FDA label covers both plaque psoriasis and active PsA, enabling full commercial promotion across both indications without restriction. EMA SmPC is aligned. No active FDA post-marketing requirements that would constrain promotional claims. The three approved key messages are fully compliant and well-supported by pivotal trial data. Minor consideration: any new promotional materials referencing head-to-head comparisons must be substantiated by adequate and well-controlled studies per FDA guidance — ensure GUIDE study references meet this threshold before deployment in branded materials. No biosimilar clock concern until at least 2029 based on patent estate.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Complete regulatory review of GUIDE head-to-head data claims for promotional use by May 2026; ensure all new PsA detail aids undergo PRC review with emphasis on joint progression claims supported by DISCOVER program data. Owner: Regulatory. Urgency: Near-term. KPI: PRC approval of updated PsA materials within 45 days of submission.

dimensions.rationale: Janssen CarePath program provides copay assistance and nurse navigator support, but ROI metrics show patient support program enrollment-to-start conversion in PsA is 62% vs. 78% in PsO — indicating friction in the PsA patient journey, likely related to more complex prior authorization pathways and longer rheumatology office onboarding times. Bridge program utilization is adequate but hub team escalation protocols for step-edit denials need strengthening — average time to appeal resolution is 18 days vs. industry benchmark of 12 days. Adherence program engagement (refill reminders, nurse check-ins) shows strong 12-month persistence signal but is not being leveraged in promotional materials to the extent the data supports. Patient advocacy engagement with National Psoriasis Foundation is strong; CreakyJoints partnership for PsA is nascent and needs acceleration.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Reduce PsA prior authorization appeal resolution time to 12 days by adding 4 dedicated PsA hub specialists by July 2026; formalize CreakyJoints co-branded PsA patient education program with launch target of September 2026; integrate 12-month persistence data into patient-facing adherence materials. Owner: Patient Support / Commercial Ops. Urgency: Immediate. KPI: PsA enrollment-to-start conversion to 72% by Q4 2026; CreakyJoints program live by September 2026.

dimensions.rationale: This is the #1 area of concern. Skyrizi is aggressively gaining share in both PsO and PsA with AbbVie's commercial machine operating at full force — Skyrizi TRx growth is outpacing Tremfya 2:1 in PsA over the last 6 months per IQVIA data. AbbVie is leveraging Humira-to-Skyrizi switch protocols as biosimilar adalimumab erodes the Humira franchise, creating a closed-loop patient capture funnel that Tremfya cannot replicate. In the IL-23 class head-to-head narrative, Skyrizi's Q12W dosing (vs. Tremfya Q8W) is being positioned by competitors as a convenience advantage — despite Tremfya's superior persistence data, the dosing frequency narrative is landing with some prescribers. Cosentyx and Taltz remain relevant in PsA given axial disease data that IL-23 class lacks. Pipeline threat: deucravacitinib (BMS) oral TYK2 inhibitor is gaining share in mild-moderate PsO and could expand to PsA, creating oral competition for biologic-naive patients. Tremfya's differentiation story — selective IL-23 p19 inhibition, durable 4-year data, favorable safety — is real but is being out-promoted.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Launch Skyrizi competitive response campaign by June 2026 repositioning Q8W as optimal dosing interval supported by pharmacokinetic drug level data and persistence outcomes; develop and deploy Humira biosimilar switch intercept program targeting gastroenterology and rheumatology practices where AbbVie is running Humira-to-Skyrizi protocols; commission rapid real-world evidence study comparing Tremfya vs. Skyrizi persistence and total cost at 18 months. Owner: Marketing + Medical Affairs joint. Urgency: Immediate. KPI: Halt Tremfya PsA TRx share erosion and stabilize at ≥15% biologic share by Q4 2026.

dimensions.rationale: Subcutaneous formulation with established cold-chain distribution through specialty pharmacy network (Accredo, AllianceRx Walgreens, BioPlus). No supply disruptions reported or anticipated. Auto-injector device has high patient satisfaction scores. Inventory levels at specialty pharmacies are within target range. No formulation changes or device updates pending that would create distribution risk. Minor flag: some rheumatology buy-and-bill offices report preference for in-office administration options that Tremfya SC self-injection does not natively address — this is not a supply issue but a channel preference consideration for PsA growth in rheumatology settings where infusion suites are standard infrastructure.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Evaluate feasibility of rheumatology in-office administration support program to reduce friction for practices accustomed to infusion-based biologics; ensure specialty pharmacy network maintains 48-hour ship-to-patient capability in top-50 PsA metro markets. Owner: Supply/Distribution + Commercial Ops. Urgency: Strategic. KPI: Maintain 98%+ on-time delivery rate; rheumatology in-office program feasibility assessment complete by Q3 2026.
